params.name: Iliad & Odyssey
params.description:
On Mar 2, 800 BC, the Hero's Epic of ancient Greece, which was the foundation of Western classical culture, was finalized.
Homer, the blind poet from ancient Greece, wrote an epic poem based on popular folk songs. "Iliad", the first part of the poem, contains 24 volumes and 15000 lines. It mainly tells the story of how the Greek coalition used wooden horses to invade Troy. "Odyssey", the second part of the poem, contains 24 volumes and 12000 lines.
